test_cases.title: Successful login with valid email and correct password redirects to dashboard
preconditions: A registered user account exists with a known valid email and correct password
The user is not currently logged in
steps: Navigate to the login page
Enter the registered user's valid email address into the email field
Enter the correct password into the password field
Submit the login form
test_cases.expected_result: The user is authenticated and redirected to their personal dashboard page

test_cases.title: Session token is created and present after successful login
preconditions: A registered user account exists with a known valid email and correct password
The user is not currently logged in
Browser developer tools or a proxy tool is available to inspect cookies/headers
steps: Navigate to the login page
Enter the registered user's valid email address into the email field
Enter the correct password into the password field
Submit the login form
Inspect the response cookies or authorization headers for a session token
test_cases.expected_result: A session token is present in the response (e.g., as a cookie or header) after successful authentication

test_cases.title: Session token is stored with secure attributes
preconditions: A registered user account exists with a known valid email and correct password
The user is not currently logged in
Browser developer tools or a proxy tool is available to inspect cookie attributes
steps: Navigate to the login page
Enter the registered user's valid email address into the email field
Enter the correct password into the password field
Submit the login form
Inspect the session token cookie attributes in the browser or intercepted response
test_cases.expected_result: The session token cookie has the Secure and HttpOnly flags set, and is transmitted only over HTTPS

test_cases.title: Login fails and no session token is created when correct email is submitted with incorrect password
preconditions: A registered user account exists with a known valid email
The user is not currently logged in
steps: Navigate to the login page
Enter the registered user's valid email address into the email field
Enter an incorrect password into the password field
Submit the login form
Inspect cookies and headers for a session token
test_cases.expected_result: The user is not authenticated, remains on the login page (or receives an error response), and no session token is created

test_cases.title: Login fails when a non-registered email is submitted
preconditions: The email address used does not correspond to any registered account
steps: Navigate to the login page
Enter an email address that is not registered in the system
Enter any password into the password field
Submit the login form
test_cases.expected_result: The user is not authenticated, is not redirected to the dashboard, and no session token is created

test_cases.title: Login form submission with empty email and password fields is rejected
preconditions: The user is not currently logged in
steps: Navigate to the login page
Leave both the email and password fields empty
Submit the login form
test_cases.expected_result: The system rejects the submission, the user is not authenticated, and no session token is created

assumptions: The session token is delivered via an HTTP cookie or an Authorization header in the login response
'Stored securely' is interpreted to mean the session token cookie has at minimum the HttpOnly and Secure flags set and is transmitted over HTTPS
The personal dashboard is a distinct page/route unique to the authenticated user
The system returns a non-success HTTP status or keeps the user on the login page when authentication fails
notes: The requirement does not specify: (1) the exact error message or HTTP status code returned on failed login, making failure-case expected results partially ambiguous; (2) the session token storage mechanism (cookie vs. localStorage vs. header), so security test cases assume cookie-based storage; (3) session expiry or token rotation behavior, which are untested; (4) whether multi-factor authentication or account lockout after repeated failures applies — these behaviors are not covered.
